g.text: adidas Digital Data has 3,032 production tables.
Today only 332 (11%) have any DQ coverage.
The 9,500 checks that exist are configured MANUALLY
by a small team -- creating a bottleneck and a fragile process.
- MTTD = 48 hours -> data issues reach consumers before detection
- SLA compliance = 60% -> 4 in 10 data products miss quality commitments
- Manual ops = 50% effort -> engineers spend half their time on toil, not value
- Onboarding = 3-6 months -> new data products take too long to get DQ coverage
- Revenue at risk -> $500K-$2M MCM revenue loss from data unavailability
No self-service. No automation. No intelligent baseline learning.
Rules require expert SQL knowledge and manual approval chains.
OK Any stakeholder creates rules in natural language -- no SQL expertise needed
OK AI-assisted SQL generation, validation and tuning
OK Automated DRE 3D detection (D1), diagnosis (D2) and deflection (D3)
OK Coverage: 11% -> 90%+ of critical Gold tables within 12 months
OK 40% toil reduction = 8-10 FTE equivalent freed for innovation
Any stakeholder can create DQ rules
via the chatbot -- not just engineers.
The platform adapts to the user role
Self-service, AI-assisted data quality management platform
on Databricks. Replaces manual, expert-only rule authoring
with natural language creation open to all stakeholders.
Rule creation -- NL chat, LLM SQL generation, trend scoring
Rule governance -- approval workflows, audit trail, versioning
DRE 3D execution -- D1 Detect, D2 Diagnose, D3 Deflect
Intelligent agents -- Profiler, Drift Classifier, Lineage Tracer,
What is the DRRI score of product X?
What is the blast radius of this anomaly?
Show rule execution results for table Y
Which tables have no DQ coverage?
asks: results, coverage gaps, blast radius
asks: DRRI score, blast radius, trend score
asks: product health KPIs, rule status
asks: audit trail, SLA history, lineage
asks: execution failures, job run status
NLP, SQL generation, Q&A, trend scoring
Stores rules, docs, versions, audit logs
D2 reads job logs, DESCRIBE HISTORY, runbooks,
code + prior ITSM incidents to build prescriptions
